1: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It boosts a core stat by exactly 0.7.
Cutting one of these in real life famously makes people cry.
It holds the very first ID number in the game's internal item list.
It gives a small increase to your tear rate.
The sprite depicts a wilted, tearful vegetable root.
2: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It slows your rate of fire in exchange for its benefit.
Its name nods to a mystical extra sense believed to grant deeper insight.
???, a secret character, has a similar marking shaped like an X on his head.
It makes you fire three shots simultaneously, though slower.
The sprite is a single staring orb perched atop Isaac's head.
3: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.
It has a notable, if odd, synergy with a popular laser-based item.
It's inspired by a famous illusionist known for warping metal utensils with his mind.
It's a required item in a challenge testing your tech skills.
Your tears curve automatically toward nearby enemies.
The sprite depicts a common kitchen utensil curved unnaturally.
4: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.
One part of its boost is a flat half-point.
It's named after one of the game creator's real pets, now living elsewhere.
It's presumed to come from the same animal as a related item, though the fur colors don't match.
It increases your damage output, both flatly and through a multiplier.
The sprite shows a small blue creature's face, floating on its own.
5: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It comes with a hidden cost: your luck stat drops.
It nods to the trope of a mirror cracking when an ugly face looks into it.

6: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It comes at a steep cost to how far your shots travel.

It increases your tear rate but drastically shortens their range.
The sprite is a giant foam finger like ones seen at sports games.
7: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.

The sprite depicts a thorned crown dripping with red.
8: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.

9: It is a Quality 0 Passive Item.

10: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.

11: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.

Its sprite is a green mushroom, just like the one that grants Mario extra lives.
12: It is a Quality 4 Passive Item.

13: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.
It shaves a bit of speed off before adding back double that amount.
Computers and living things can both catch one of these.
Picking it up makes Isaac resemble Lust, who also drops it.
Touching enemies poisons them on contact.
Poisoned enemies killed this way have a chance to drop a black heart.
14: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.

Its name describes mood swings often blamed on steroid abuse.
A newer item themed after mercury now holds the speed-boost record it once had.
It significantly increases your movement speed and shot range, with your tears also falling faster.
Before Repentance, it was the single biggest speed increase of any item in the game.
15: It is a Quality 2 Passive Item.

17: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.

18: It is a Quality 3 Passive Item.

31: It is a Quality 1 Passive Item.

34: It is a Quality 2 Active Item.
